tip: Use `ci"` to change text inside quotes without moving your cursor to the start.
Use `zz` to center the current line in the window â€” great after jumping to a search result.
`*` searches forward for the word under your cursor. `#` searches backward.
Use `gf` to open the file whose path is under the cursor.
`<C-o>` jumps back to your previous cursor position. `<C-i>` jumps forward.
Use `va{` to visually select everything including the curly braces, or `vi{` for inside only.
`gg=G` auto-indents the entire file. Useful for quick formatting.
Use `:set wrap!` to toggle line wrapping without leaving the editor.
`q:` opens the command history window. Navigate and re-run past commands.
In insert mode, `<C-r>"` pastes from the default register without leaving insert mode.
Use `useAsyncData` with a unique key per page to avoid cache collisions between routes.
`useFetch` is shorthand for `useAsyncData` + `$fetch`. Use `useFetch` for simple cases, `useAsyncData` when you need more control.
Place shared composables in `~/composables/` â€” Nuxt auto-imports them with no import statement needed.
Use `definePageMeta({ layout: 'myLayout' })` inside a page to switch layouts without wrapping components.
`useRuntimeConfig()` exposes public config on client + server, and private keys server-side only. Never put secrets in `public`.
The `~/server/api/` directory creates API routes automatically. Export a default `defineEventHandler` function.
Use `<NuxtLink>` instead of `<a>` for internal links â€” it prefetches the route on hover.
`useState` persists state between pages on the client. Useful as a lightweight alternative to Pinia for simple shared state.
Use `--no-cache` flag with `docker build` to force a clean rebuild when debugging layer issues.
Multi-stage builds keep your final image small: build in one stage, copy only the artifact to the next.
`docker exec -it <container> sh` opens a shell inside a running container for quick debugging.
Put frequently changing lines (like `COPY . .`) at the bottom of your Dockerfile to maximize layer cache reuse.
Use `.dockerignore` to exclude `node_modules`, `.git`, and build artifacts â€” it speeds up build context transfer.
`docker compose watch` (Compose v2.22+) auto-rebuilds on file changes during development.
Use named volumes for persistent data (`docker volume create`) rather than bind mounts in production.
`git stash push -m 'description'` names your stash so `git stash list` stays readable.
Use `git log --oneline --graph --all` for a compact visual view of all branches.
`git commit --fixup <sha>` creates a fixup commit. Later, `git rebase -i --autosquash` merges them automatically.
`git bisect start` + `git bisect good/bad` does a binary search through commits to find which one introduced a bug.
Use `git restore --staged <file>` to unstage a file without discarding your working tree changes.
`git diff --stat HEAD~1` shows which files changed in the last commit with a line count summary.
Use `!!` to repeat the last command. `sudo !!` is the fastest way to re-run with sudo.
`<C-r>` starts a fuzzy history search. If you have fzf installed, it becomes a full interactive picker.
Use `take dirname` (oh-my-zsh) to create a directory and immediately `cd` into it.
Global aliases like `alias -g G='| grep'` let you write `ls G pattern` anywhere in a command.
`cd -` jumps back to the previous directory. Combine with `pushd`/`popd` for a directory stack.
